questions.question: Which of the following correctly uses the present simple tense?
choices: The earth goes round the sun.
I will go away at weekends.
They teached in a school yesterday.
questions.correct_answer: The earth goes round the sun.
questions.explanation: The present simple tense is used to express general truths or habitual actions. 'The earth goes round the sun' is a general truth statement.

questions.question: How is the negative form of the present simple tense formed?
questions.explanation: To form the negative in the present simple tense, we use the auxiliary verb 'don't' + the base form of the main verb.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a correct use of the present simple tense?
choices: I promise I won't be late.
Julie doesn't drink tea very often.
Robert usually goes away two or three times a year.
We are going to the dentist next week.
questions.correct_answer: We are going to the dentist next week.
questions.explanation: The present simple is not used for scheduled future events. The present continuous is more appropriate for this case.

questions.question: Which verb form is used with 'he/she/it' in the present simple tense?
questions.explanation: For the third-person singular (he/she/it), we add an 's' to the base form of the verb in the present simple tense.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of using the present simple to express habitual actions?
choices: She will drive the bus tomorrow.
questions.explanation: The present simple tense is used to express habitual or repeated actions. 'I work in a shop' expresses a habitual action.

questions.question: Which modal verb is commonly used to make suggestions in the present simple tense?
questions.explanation: The modal verb 'should' is commonly used to make suggestions in the present simple tense, as in 'I suggest that you should study more.'

questions.question: Which of the following correctly uses the present simple to talk about frequency?
choices: Robert goes away two or three times a year.
I am getting up at 8 o'clock every morning.
She will drive the bus tomorrow.
questions.correct_answer: Robert goes away two or three times a year.
questions.explanation: The present simple tense can be used to express how often an action occurs. 'Robert goes away two or three times a year' expresses the frequency of an action.

questions.question: Which verb form is used with 'I/you/we/they' in the present simple tense?
questions.explanation: For the first-person (I), second-person (you), and plural subjects (we/they), we use the base form of the verb in the present simple tense.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of using the present simple to express general truths?
choices: Nurses look after patients in hospitals.
I usually go away at weekends.
The café opens at 7:30 in the morning.
Rice doesn't grow in cold climates.
questions.correct_answer: Nurses look after patients in hospitals.
questions.explanation: The present simple tense is used to express general truths or facts that are always true. 'Nurses look after patients in hospitals' is a general truth statement.

questions.question: Which verb form is used to make questions in the present simple tense?
questions.explanation: To form questions in the present simple tense, we use the auxiliary verbs 'do/does' followed by the base form of the main verb.
